tool.name: SameStr Merge
tool.description: nucleotide variant profiles obtained from metagenomic samples for filtering and pairwise comparisons
tool.command:
#for $file in $input_files: ln -s '$file' '${file.element_identifier}.${file.ext}' && #end for
samestr merge --marker-dir '$database.fields.path' --output-dir out_merge/ --input-files #for $file in $input_files: '${file.element_identifier}.${file.ext}' #end for #if $clade: --clade '$clade' #end if

param.name: input_files
param.label: Input SNV profiles
param.help: Single-sample/-genome SNV profile datasets generated SameStr Convert or Extract.

param.name: database
param.label: SameStr database
param.help: SameStr database built from MetaPhlAn or mOTUS markers

param.label: Clade to process from input profiles.
param.help: Processing all if not specified. Names must correspond to the taxonomy of the respective database [e.g. t__SGB10068 for MetaPhlAn]

tool.help:
@SAMESTR_INTRO@
SameStr Merge .............
SameStr Merge combines nucleotide variant profiles obtained from multiple metagenomic samples with SameStr Convert, and/or from reference genomes with Samestr Extract for subsequent filtering and pairwise strain comparisons.
**Input Requirements**
SameStr Merge processes multiple SNV profiles simultaneously and the following inputs must be provided:
- One or more single-sample / single-genome SNV profiles in NPZ/NPY format generated by Samestr Convert or Samestr Extract. - The MetaPhlAn or mOTUS clade marker database that matches the database used to generate the input profiles.
**Parameters**
- Clade to process: Specify a particular clade to merge. If no clade is specified, the tool will process all clades.
**Results and Outputs**
The tool generates two outputs:
- A collection of per-clade SNV profiles merged across samples in compressed NumPy format (npz). The collection will have one element per clade. - A collection of lists of samples per clade with the same number of elements as the SNV profiles collection. Each element lists, in simple text format, the names of the samples that are included in the corresponding merged SNV profile.
